name: PptxGenJS (PowerPoint Generation for JavaScript)

performance.notes: Generation time proportional to slide count and media content

agent_readiness.notes: PowerPoint (.pptx) file generation for Node.js and browser. import PptxGenJS from 'pptxgenjs'. const pptx = new PptxGenJS(). const slide = pptx.addSlide(). slide.addText('Hello World', { x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 1, fontSize: 36, color: 'FF0000' }). slide.addImage({ path: './logo.png', x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 2, h: 1 }). slide.addShape(pptx.ShapeType.rect, { x: 0, y: 0, w: 10, h: 0.5, fill: '1D4ED8' }). slide.addTable([[{text: 'A', options: {bold: true}}, 'B'], ['1', '2']], {x: 1, y: 2}). slide.addChart(pptx.ChartType.bar, [{name:'Series1', labels:['Q1','Q2'], values:[10,20]}]). pptx.writeFile({fileName: 'report.pptx'}). pptx.write({outputType: 'base64'}). Master slides. Section layout.

gotchas: Coordinate system is in inches — X, Y, W, H all in inches (10 x 7.5 default slide)
Browser: CORS required for remote image paths; use base64 instead
Complex animations and transitions not supported
Large presentations with many images can be slow
best_when: Automated report generation as PowerPoint slides
Data visualization slides with charts from JSON data
Node.js server-side PPTX generation for download
avoid_when: When Google Slides API or LibreOffice UNO is available for editing
summary: PptxGenJS generates PowerPoint (.pptx) files. pptx.addSlide().addText(), addImage(), addShape(), addChart(), addTable(). writeFile() / write(base64). MIT by Brent Ely. Type-annotated. Works in Node.js and browser. Best for automated PPTX report generation. Write-only — no parsing. Coordinates in inches. Complex animations not supported. Charts from JSON data.
